author.description: Welcome to the Official Rapid Response Account of the Department of War.

tweets.text:
.@SECWAR “On this sacred occasion for our Nation, we take this day to remember our very best.

They now lie in silent rows here at Arlington, in more than 170 national cemeteries throughout our Nation, and across 17 countries around the world.

They answered the call when it mattered—and they gave ‘the last full measure of devotion’ so that this great Republic of ours might endure.”
tweets.text_resolved:
.@SECWAR “On this sacred occasion for our Nation, we take this day to remember our very best.

They answered the call when it mattered—and they gave ‘the last full measure of devotion’ so that this great Republic of ours might endure.”

author.description: Welcome to the Official Rapid Response Account of the Department of War.

tweets.text:
.@SECWAR “This day began as ‘Decoration Day,’ when widows, orphans, and other mourners placed flowers and ribbons on some 20,000 graves…

The mourners on that first Decoration Day knew what we know on this Memorial Day—That these graves are more than names and dates with a dash in between.

Each grave marker is a story.”
tweets.text_resolved:

AMERICAN BLOOD.” https://t.co/11ckZeJC9U

author.description: Welcome to the Official Rapid Response Account of the Department of War.

tweets.text:
.@SECWAR “They fought not because they hate what was in front of them, but because they loved what was behind them.

And, so, may the ones we remember today live on in every flag that flies… 

May they live in our prayers to Almighty God." https://t.co/6uofxpt8ka

author.description: Welcome to the Official Rapid Response Account of the Department of War.

tweets.text:
.@SECWAR “In the words of the Apostle Paul, they stood against the darkness of the world wearing the breastplate of righteousness—They’ve now gone on to their reward, but we remember them here, on this hallowed ground.

To them, to those, I say: Your watch is finished, but the legacy of what you left is eternal.”
tweets.text_resolved:
.@SECWAR “In the words of the Apostle Paul, they stood against the darkness of the world wearing the breastplate of righteousness—They’ve now gone on to their reward, but we remember them here, on this hallowed ground.

author.description: Welcome to the Official Rapid Response Account of the Department of War.

tweets.text:
.@POTUS “Before we hail the founding—we honor the fallen. Before we celebrate the triumph—we pay tribute. Before we crown the victory—we count the cost.

Today, we are reminded that there could be no Fourth of July without America’s Armed Forces, and there could be no Independence Day without Memorial Day.

We owe our liberty, ourselves, our self-government, the glories of our history, and the very nature of our nation itself to the generations who paid for it with EVERYTHING THEY HAD—THE ULTIMATE SACRIFICE.”
tweets.text_resolved:
.@POTUS “Before we hail the founding—we honor the fallen. Before we celebrate the triumph—we pay tribute. Before we crown the victory—we count the cost.

We owe our liberty, ourselves, our self-government, the glories of our history, and the very nature of our nation itself to the generations who paid for it with EVERYTHING THEY HAD—THE ULTIMATE SACRIFICE.”

author.description: Welcome to the Official Rapid Response Account of the Department of War.

tweets.text:
.@POTUS “In two hundred and fifty years, America’s heroes have saved more lives, freed more captives, accomplished more good, and spread more hope than any other people at any time in the history of the world.

Whole nations now know liberty. Entire generations know freedom. Billions of people have been delivered from poverty, tyranny, and oppression.

Because of the sacrifices, we honor this day.”
